tool_args.label: Queens Blvd Line West CBTC (QBL-W)

payload.description: CBTC from Union Turnpike in Queens through 50 St/8 Av on the 53 St Line and 21 St/Queensbridge on the 63 St Line. Includes equipping 335 R-160 units. First interoperable CBTC project for NYCT.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Queens Blvd Line West CBTC (QBL-W)

tool_args.label: 8th Avenue Line CBTC

payload.description: CBTC from 59 St in Manhattan through High St in Brooklyn. Includes Axle Counter System, R-179 and R-211 CBTC equipment. Serves A, C, E lines. More than 710,000 daily riders pre-COVID.

evidence_refs.source_quote: 8 Av Line CBTC

tool_args.label: Culver Line CBTC

payload.description: CBTC between West 8 St and Church Av in Brooklyn. 4.7 route miles, 12 subway stations. Contractor Tutor Perini, supplier Siemens.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Culver Line CBTC

tool_args.label: Queens Blvd Line East CBTC (QBL-E)

payload.description: CBTC from north of Union Turnpike to 179 St Station on the Queens Blvd (Hillside Ave) Line (F). Interoperability to include Mitsubishi.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Queens Blvd Line East CBTC (QBL- E)

tool_args.label: Crosstown Line CBTC (CBTC Centric)

payload.description: First CBTC Centric project and first Design-Build project for S&TC Business Unit. G line from Court Square to Church Avenue. Uses 5G technology.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Crosstown Communications Based Train Control (CBTC) Design-Build Project

tool_args.label: First CBTC section placed in revenue service on Culver Line

payload.description: First CBTC section (S1) placed in revenue service on Culver Line after several delays caused by software/database issues.

evidence_refs.source_quote: Culver CBTC in-service testing and commissioning is scheduled to begin with Section S1 in March 2024
A first CBTC section has been successfully put in revenue service

tool_args.label: Congestion Pricing litigation causing hold on new construction contracts

payload.claim_text: Due to the risk of ongoing litigation delaying congestion pricing funding the MTA plans to commit $2.9 billion worth of capital projects in 2024. Uncertainty surrounding the lawsuits have shrunk C&D's 2024 Commitment Plan to only $2.9 billion dollars, instead of the average of $9 billion per year over the last three years.
payload.subject: MTA C&D Capital Program
payload.description: Congestion Pricing litigation causing hold on new construction contract solicitations and reducing 2024 commitments

evidence_refs.source_quote: uncertainty surrounding the lawsuits have shrunk C&D's 2024 Commitment Plan to only $2.9 billion dollars, instead of the average of $9 billion per year over the last three years

tool_args.label: Congestion Pricing litigation hold impacts on projects

payload.claim_text: This hold will affect key projects including ADA expansions in all 5 boroughs and CBTC upgrades on the Fulton A/C line in Brooklyn, as well as the 6 Av line across the B, D, F, and M lines throughout 4 boroughs. Also at risk is another $1 billion in funding for new subway cars, rail cars, and the purchase of 270 electric buses and the installation of charging infrastructure at 10 depots.
payload.subject: MTA C&D Capital Program

evidence_refs.source_quote: hold will affect key projects including ADA expansions in all 5 boroughs and CBTC upgrades on the Fulton A/C line in Brooklyn, as well as the 6 Av line
purchase of 270 electric buses and the installation of charging infrastructure at 10 depots

issues: evidence_refs[0].source_quote is not present in the cited block

tool_args.label: Individual Disability Exemption Plan (IDEP) created for congestion pricing

payload.claim_text: the MTA has created the Individual Disability Exemption Plan (IDEP). Under this plan, individuals who qualify for IDEP may register one vehicle that belongs to the applicant, a family member, or a caregiver, as exempt.
payload.subject: MTA Congestion Pricing

evidence_refs.source_quote: MTA has created the Individual Disability Exemption Plan (IDEP)

tool_args.label: Congestion Pricing litigation hold impacts on projects

payload.claim_text: while litigation is pending, President Torres-Springer announced that with very few exceptions C&D will not issue any new construction contract solicitations. This hold will affect key projects including ADA expansions in all 5 boroughs and CBTC upgrades on the Fulton A/C line in Brooklyn, as well as the 6 Av line across the B, D, F, and M lines throughout 4 boroughs. Also at risk, is another $1 billion in funding for new subway cars, rail cars, and the purchase of 270 electric buses and the installation of charging infrastructure at 10 depots.
payload.subject: MTA C&D Capital Program

evidence_refs.source_quote: while litigation is pending, President Torres-Springer announced that with very few exceptions C&D will not issue any new construction contract solicitations
